title: Middleborough Dairy Queen sells for more than $4 million
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Middleborough's Dairy Queen location sold for $4.2 million to the owner's nieces, retaining family ownership.
The location ranks as the second-highest-grossing 'Treat Only' Dairy Queen in the U.S. and the most visited in Massachusetts.
The store has been family-owned since 1966, passing from John Pennini to his son George, now to nieces Nicole and Kara.

title: Kane’s Donuts debuts World Cup-themed doughnuts to crown fans’ favorite team
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Kane's Donuts launches World Cup-themed doughnuts representing different countries each week ahead of the tournament.
The first doughnut round features Scotland and Haiti, decorated in their national flag colors for fans to choose from.
Kane's Donuts locations in Saugus and Boston will offer new country-themed doughnuts throughout the World Cup.

title: Court docs: Suspected Cambridge gunman waved rifle at parole officer over FaceTime prior to shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Brown allegedly waved a rifle on FaceTime with his parole officer before the Cambridge shooting on Memorial Drive.
Brown, previously diagnosed with PTSD, shot two men in their vehicles, causing life-threatening injuries, police said.
A Massachusetts State Trooper and a civilian fired back, injuring Brown and preventing further violence, according to authorities.

title: 2 N.H. residents were on cruise ship that quarantined over hantavirus, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two New Hampshire residents were on the MV Hondius cruise ship during a hantavirus outbreak.
Three passengers have died from the outbreak, according to the World Health Organization.
The U.S. typically reports about 30 hantavirus cases annually; public risk from this incident is low.

title: Red Sox might have to make a decision on Trevor Story amid veteran’s struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Trevor Story is struggling with a .200 batting average, 55 strikeouts in 155 at-bats, and a .520 OPS.
The Red Sox consider shifting Story to second base and starting Marcelo Mayer at shortstop to improve defense.
Boston may offload Story's $50 million contract as his performance declines, possibly through trade or assignment.

title: Lynn mother, educator avoids deportation for now
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mariola, a Lynn educator from Guatemala, is temporarily safe from deportation after an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) check-in.
Mariola will undergo a 'Reasonable Fear Interview' to assess her eligibility for asylum or fear-based relief in the U.S.
She has community support, including from Lynn Mayor Jared Nicholson, against her deportation due to her indigenous Mayan background.

title: Night Shift’s outdoor beer gardens are back for the 2026 season. Here’s what to know.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Night Shift Brewing's beer gardens reopen this week at the Esplanade, Herter Park, and Dewey Square.
New drinks include Rickey Sour, Mai Day, and Double Parked; regular offerings are also available.
Food trucks will rotate at the Esplanade and Allston locations, which are walk-in and dog-friendly.

title: Sail Boston 250 is coming up. Here’s where to learn to sail.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 250 will feature a fleet of tall ships from over 25 countries sailing into Boston Harbor, July 11-16.
Boston area sailing schools offer courses for all skill levels, including Beginner Mercury Classes and advanced options for adults.
Sailing centers like Boston Sailing Center and Courageous Sailing provide certified courses to prepare students for independent sailing.

title: Suspected Memorial Drive gunman’s criminal record comes under scrutiny after Cambridge shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Brown, 46, suspected of firing over 50 rounds on Memorial Drive, was on probation for a 2020 offense.
Brown faces multiple charges, including armed assault and weapons charges, and is in custody at a Boston hospital.
The incident has sparked criticism of lenient sentencing by Massachusetts judges and parole board officials.

title: Get one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest for a limited time
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Southwest Airlines offers one-way flights from $59 during a four-day sale ending May 14 at 11:59 p.m. PT.
Discounts apply to select domestic flights on Tuesdays and Wednesdays and international flights on Sundays to Wednesdays.
Boston deals include $59 flights to Baltimore and $197 to Atlanta, valid for travel between specific dates in 2026.
